awareness: unaware of the scale of automation - fear of being made redundant.
desire: will reject the portal if cases return with no context, or if they think portal automates them out of work.
knowledge: how to interpret case context from the portal, how to prioritise cases when flagged, what decisions they can make vs. what needs escalation
ability: if context is only partially complete, if flags are inconsistent or confusing, if escalation pathway is unclear in the workflow.
reinforcement: if delinquent account statuses keep progressing with the portal, they will lose confidence.

awareness: if they dont know the portal exists, they will overload agent lines, thus making the portal redundant.
desire: if the portal feels undignified/impersonal, bureaucratic, or slow, customers won't use it.
knowledge: portal need to be easy to use and they need to know what their payment options are and what happens if they miss a payment ie. disciplinary pathway
ability: buggy or non-intuitive website, indecision in selecting payment option from a lack of information, and a gap between the disciplinary procedure and what actions they need to take.
reinforcement: if portal is too complex, slow, or impersonal; also if they feel like payment selection and scheduling from the algorithms are unfair and they arent given the option to challenge them

awareness: they will see the effect (15% revenue loss) but not the effect.
desire: some cases need to stay agent-led for compliance purposes. they may also dismiss improvements to operational efficiency if financial statements come back the same.
knowledge: need assumptions that are backed into revenue projection.
ability: need to be able to understand the holistic picture enough to validate the assumptions we give them.
reinforcement: if the portal does not show an increase in revenue, or assumptions do not hold, they will lose confidence in the portal.
